what_it_does: This file is an interpretive ledger entry for the claude-ds source file app/ai/ai-registry.js. It stores a structured reading of that module: a summary of what it does, lists of observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, questions, themes, novelty, contribution, and embedding-ready sentences. It also records that no code symbols were extracted from the source.
observations: The file is a single JSON object following an interpretive ledger schema with project, path, model, and descriptive fields.
It documents window.CV_AI as a five-layer AI registry (provider, behaviour, skill, capability, context) whose API mirrors CV_REGISTRY.
It lists runtime-resolution rules: claude needs window.claude.complete, openai-image needs window.cvOpenAI, and other providers delegate to window.CV_HOST.resolveProviderRuntime.
It notes that execute() is the single generative path ai-output = f(capability, context, params).
It records persistence split between in-memory built-ins and localStorage-persisted user entries.
It marks the symbols array as empty, indicating no code symbols were extracted.
It includes both a summary_for_embedding and an intention_for_embedding sentence.
standouts: It is a meta-document: an interpretation and evaluation of a source module rather than source code itself.
It encodes design intent, API shape, and behavioral contracts in a queryable, structured form.
It provides embedding-ready one-line summaries of both content and purpose.
conventions: Follows the interpretive-layer schema with required keys and string-array fields.
Uses neutral, descriptive language for most fields while allowing evaluative inferences in notes and purpose_vs_actual.
Represents absent code symbols with an empty symbols array.
concerns: Some claims, such as purpose_vs_actual 'Match' and references to 'the project's non-negotiables', cannot be verified from this file alone.
The empty symbols list means the entry contains no grounded code-level facts; it is entirely a high-level reading.
Concerns about localStorage and notify swallowing exceptions describe the source module, not this JSON file, and their provenance is not shown here.
notes: This JSON is an interpretive ledger entry for ai-registry.js, packaging a design-level reading, API summary, and embedding sentences with no extracted code symbols.
questions: Was this interpretation generated from the actual source of ai-registry.js, from a header comment, or from a prompt description?
What does the model field 'opus' refer to, and how was it chosen for this entry?
Why is the symbols array empty — was extraction skipped or did the source lack extractable symbols?
How are the evaluative judgments, such as 'purpose_vs_actual': 'Match', validated?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the file presents itself as an interpretive ledger entry and contains the expected interpretive fields, summaries, and an empty symbols list.
intention_signals: Trying to produce a machine-readable design summary of the AI registry module so downstream fusion, search, or review can understand its behavior and API without reading the source directly.
novelty: It packages both factual description and inferred intention signals into a structured, embedding-ready ledger entry for a single code module.
contribution: Contributes a compact, queryable interpretive record of ai-registry.js, including its mirrored registry API, execute() generative path, provider and context resolution behavior, noted concerns, and embedding summaries; the empty symbols list signals that no code-level symbol facts were captured.
summary_for_embedding: An interpretive ledger JSON for claude-ds app/ai/ai-registry.js, capturing its CV_REGISTRY-shaped AI registry design, execute() generative path, provider and context resolution behavior, observations, concerns, and embedding summaries, with no code symbols.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a machine-readable design reading of the AI registry module for downstream fusion, search, and understanding.
